mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing three months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following declines are not statistically higher than baseline (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, it signals deteriorating labor conditions that precede earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger fires, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Joint weakness in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) - both declining simultaneously over two consecutive months - indicates a broad-based consumer and construction demand slowdown that compounds into a stronger negative equity signal than either series alone, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Joint decline episodes show forward 60d returns not significantly different from single-series decline episodes (interaction effect not additive), or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising month-over-month trend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding real economic output and corporate revenue capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week as markets price in improving fundamentals.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO trailing 1-month change and forward 5d returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 50,000 jobs, occurring while UNRATE is simultaneously above 4.5%, signals an entrenched labor contraction regime in which equity markets systematically underprice recession risk, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: early recessionary regime with elevated unemployment
failure_condition: During triggered periods, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect disappears when UNRATE condition is removed, indicating no regime dependence.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between elevated FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL captures a real-rate tightening regime: when nominal rates rise faster than inflation, real borrowing costs increase, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0 AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change < FEDFUNDS level
regime_hint: monetary tightening with rising real rates
failure_condition: Forward 60d returns in the joint-trigger regime are not significantly lower than in periods where only FEDFUNDS rises without the real-rate condition, indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month trend in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects sustained consumer spending strength, which supports near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS 1-month change and forward 5d returns falls below 0.02 across any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO growth turns negative for two consecutive months during a period of elevated GDPC1 growth deceleration, it signals an approaching industrial recession that late-cycle equity markets have not yet priced, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter, an effect that is strongest in late-expansion regimes but absent in early-recovery regimes.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO m/m growth negative for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating GDP growth
failure_condition: The negative-return effect is equally strong or stronger in early-recovery regimes as in late-cycle regimes, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows restricted to late-cycle periods.
